mission.title: spire: spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request / Enhancement for node attestation
mission.description: spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request / Enhancement for node attestation . Requested by 4+ users.

steps.title: Check current spire deployment
steps.description:
Verify your spire version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
helm list -n spire 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working spire installation.

steps.title: Review spire configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get configmap -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=spire
```
Hello Team 👋 
Hope you are well.

We are looking for a feature, where in we add one more check to node attestation flow.

steps.title: Apply the fix for spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request /
steps.description:
This PR adds new feature to AWS Node Attester Plugin. It's optional feature, if its enabled it provides additional check executed at very first step during node attestation. It verifies, if node's `aws account id` is part of `AWS Organization` or not. If it's not part of organization, attestation
```yaml
account_ids_belong_to_org_validation = {
        org_account_id     = "7891011"
        org_account_role   = "spire-server-org-role"
        org_account_region = "us-west-2"
        org_account_map_ttl = "6"
      }
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get events -n spire --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "spire-server: [AWS Node Attestor] Feature Request /" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This PR adds new feature to AWS Node Attester Plugin. It's optional feature, if its enabled it provides additional check executed at very first step during node attestation. It verifies, if node's `aws account id` is part of `AWS Organization` or not. If it's not part of organization, attestation request will be rejected.
codeSnippets:
org_account_role   = "spire-server-org-role" <---- use same role for node attestation 
        org_account_region = "us-west-2" <---- default region we agree on
        org_account_map_ttl = "6" <--- default ttl if we go with existing route

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
